input_text:
Users were able to over-order limited inventory items. Two users would simultaneously check "is item X available?" (yes, 1 left), both proceed to checkout, both successfully place orders, now we have -1 inventory.

Classic read-modify-write race condition. The fix: use SELECT FOR UPDATE when reading inventory during checkout. This places a row-level lock — the second user's SELECT waits until the first transaction commits or rolls back.

This is now the pattern for all inventory reservation operations.
reviewer_notes: Resolved race condition fixed with SELECT FOR UPDATE. While it does establish a forward pattern, nothing was tried-and-rejected — it's completed remediation.
